claim: The test suite shall provide a fast end-to-end signal that the framework is healthy on the real domain graph -- load the graph, run every generator template, and regenerate docs without panicking or empty output.
why: An agent after a change should not need to remember the full test count or layout -- one signal means 'the framework did not fall over on real data'. A distributed set of tests together covers that ground: TestGenSpec_SmokeOnRealDomain (internal/generator/byteidentical_test.go) loads the real hotam-spec-self domain graph (domains/hotam-spec-self/graph.json) and runs every generator Build* template (REQUIREMENTS/TENSIONS/OPEN/UNENFORCED/GLOSSARY/HISTORY/DECISIONS/CONSTITUTION/ENTITIES/FRAMEWORK-INVARIANTS/REPO-MAP/live-state plus BuildGraphJSON), asserting non-empty output and no panic; the all-violations coverage is exercised by internal/invariants' own real-graph tests; the harness path is exercised by internal/diagnose integration tests (TestAllFindings_RealGraphNoPanic) and the cmd/hotam CLI tests. The single-test shape is split across packages because tests run per-package, but the collective signal is the same one-health-check intent. (Historical note: this role was originally one end-to-end test, spec/tests/test_smoke.py.) PROSE (not ENFORCED): no single check_* gates this; it is the test suite's role, not an invariant. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestSmoke_EveryBuildTemplateOnRealDomainNoPanicNoEmpty in internal/generator/byteidentical_test.go mechanically verifies this claim.
